message.reasoning:
We need to follow instructions: Output ONLY the UCL command(s), no explanations or markdown. Must return JSON with shape: {"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}. Use null or omit tool_call unless kind is tool_call. When kind is tool_call, tool_name must exactly match one of the registered tool names. Do not invent tools.

We are in state: Task: Gather the exact evidence needed before editing schema definitions ... Class: prepare_to_edit. Active code windows: artifacts.rs with constants. Graph relations none. Frontier recommends expand_file [F1] with priority 112. So likely we need to perform a tool call to expand_file? Actually expand_file is a session command: session.expand_file. That is a registered tool name: session.expand_file@v1. So we should issue a tool_call with tool_name "session.expand_file@v1" and payload for target selector etc. Need to decide payload. The instructions say use exact block IDs as provided; block IDs are short numeric IDs (1,2,3...). But we have no IDs given. We can maybe target the file using its path? The payload likely expects a selector string. Could be something like "file:crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". Use that.

We also need to include acquired_fact_ids: an array of fact ids. We haven't acquired any facts yet, maybe empty array? But schema requires array of strings. Could be empty list []? It says acquired_fact_ids:["<fact id>"]; maybe can be empty? Probably can be [] (empty array). Use empty array as JSON array.

Readiness_state: likely "evidence_visible" or "ready_to_edit"? The instruction: "Gather the exact evidence needed before editing schema definitions". So we have evidence visible but not yet edited. So readiness_state maybe "evidence_visible". Use that.

assistant_message: brief status, e.g., "expanding file symbols". Keep short.

kind: "tool_call" because we are making a tool call.

We must output JSON only, no markdown.

Structure: {"kind":"tool_call","assistant_message":"expanding file symbols","tool_call":{"tool_name":"session.expand_file@v1","payload":{...}},"acquired_fact_ids":[],"readiness_state":"evidence_visible"}

Now payload: need to include target selector. Possibly "crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". Use selector string. Also optional depth, limit, etc. Not required but can include some. Provide minimal: maybe just "target":"crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". But payload keys? Not specified. Probably just a JSON object with selector. Use "target":"crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". Provide no other fields.

Thus payload: {"target":"crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs"}

Make sure JSON is valid.

Now final output: ensure no extra whitespace? It's okay.

Let's craft:

{
  "kind":"tool_call",
  "assistant_message":"expanding file symbols",
  "tool_call":{
    "tool_name":"session.expand_file@v1",
    "payload":{
      "target":"crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs"
    }
  },
  "acquired_fact_ids":[],
  "readiness_state":"evidence_visible"
}

Check: need exact shape: "{{"kind":"think|tool_call|complete","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{{"tool_name":"<one registered tool name>","payload":{{}}}},"acquired_fact_ids":["<fact id>"],"readiness_state":"not_ready|evidence_visible|evidence_acquired|ready_to_edit"}}.

Our payload is object, not array. That's fine.

acquired_fact_ids is array; we gave [].

All good.

Now output exactly that JSON, no markdown.
